text:
🔭 Subagents and agent swarms

A skill injects a reusable prompt into the current context window (cheap, synchronous). A subagent spawns an isolated context with its own tools + permissions (expensive, enables parallelism, protects parent context). An agent swarm = multiple subagents concurrently, either under a central supervisor (safe, debuggable) or peer-to-peer (emergent).

Key points:
• Use a skill when behavior is reusable and output fits inline — no isolation needed [Fact / Spring AI]
• Spawn a subagent when: context explosion risk, parallel execution needed, permission boundaries required, or specialized toolset [Fact / VILA-Lab]
• Supervisor architecture = safe default for enterprise; peer-to-peer swarm suits open-ended tasks with dynamic routing [Practitioner consensus]
• Anthropic's internal research system: lead researcher + parallel SearchAgents → 90% time reduction, 15× token cost (reported, unverified primary source)
